title: triantha occidentalis subsp. montana
intro: Triantha occidentalis subsp. montana, also known as the Western Thimbleberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of salicylic acid. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues. The Western Thimbleberry can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
